tasks_completed.description: FIX CRITICAL-2: Force is_regex=False for --tool flag branch in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh
tasks_completed.changes: Changed `is_regex = _looks_regex(flag_pattern)` to `is_regex = False` in the `if flag_pattern is not None:` branch. The --tool flag is an explicit literal declaration; the _looks_regex() heuristic must not override it for dotted filenames like /tmp/file.json which are misclassified as regex patterns (they contain a dot).
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: _looks_regex() classifies any string containing '.' as a regex. Dotted file paths like /tmp/file.json trigger IS_REGEX=true, causing the sentinel write block to be skipped entirely (by design for regex grants). Fix: force is_regex=False unconditionally when --tool is present.

tasks_completed.description: FIX CRITICAL-1: Detect bare /allow Write /path form and emit scoped sentinel instead of wildcard
tasks_completed.changes: Added `elif first == 'Write' and len(bare) >= 2 and bare[1].startswith('/'):` branch in the `elif bare:` block. When triggered, sets `pattern = 'Write ' + bare[1]` and `is_regex = False`. Also added `comment = ' '.join(bare[1:])` to the `re:` branch to preserve correct comment assignment after refactoring comment assignments into each sub-branch. Critical additional fix: removed double-quote characters from Python comments embedded in bash `python3 -c "..."` block — double quotes in comments like `# Bare "/allow Write /path" form:` caused bash to terminate the double-quoted string early, making the entire Python block produce no output and fall through to the wildcard default.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: two-token bare syntax (`/allow Write /tmp/file.json`) puts the path in bare[1], which the old code treated as a comment token. The resulting sentinel was `{op:'Write'}` (wildcard), granting unconditional Write to any path. Fix: detect the two-token Write+path pattern before falling into the default bare handler.

tasks_completed.description: FIX ORIGINAL: Sentinel writer emits 'target' field for Write ops instead of 'args_contain'
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced `if rest: entry['args_contain'] = [rest]` with key-presence logic: `if op == 'Write' and rest: entry['target'] = rest` / `elif rest: entry['args_contain'] = [rest]`. Write ops now emit `target` field that the matcher checks exactly.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: schema mismatch. The writer always emitted `args_contain`, but the matcher key-presence check on `target` caused it to fall through to the else-wildcard branch, granting unconditional Write regardless of target path. Fix: Write ops emit `target`, non-Write ops emit `args_contain`.

tasks_completed.description: FIX ORIGINAL: match_sentinel_grant_for_write uses key-presence branching instead of .get() wildcard fallthrough
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced `entry_target = entry.get('target') / if entry_target is None or entry_target == target_path: return entry` with key-presence branching: if 'target' in entry -> exact string equality; elif 'args_contain' in entry -> exact one-item list equality (legacy schema fallback); else -> wildcard (intentional bare {op:Write} grant). Updated docstring to reflect corrected semantics.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: `entry.get('target')` returns None when key is absent, and `None == target_path` is False but `entry_target is None` is True, causing the condition `entry_target is None or entry_target == target_path` to match any entry without a target field. This turned every `args_contain`-schema entry into a wildcard. Fix: key-presence branching is unambiguous.

tasks_completed.description: Add AC4-AC8 test methods to TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite in test_allowlist_consolidation.py
tasks_completed.changes: Added 5 new test methods: test_ac4_legacy_args_contain_denies_other_path, test_ac5_exploit_shape_regression_no_wildcard_leakage, test_ac6_bare_write_sentinel_wildcard_preserved, test_ac7_e2e_tool_flag_dotted_path_sentinel (subprocess), test_ac8_e2e_bare_syntax_scoped_sentinel (subprocess). Also added `import subprocess` to imports.
tasks_completed.rationale: TDD: tests written to capture acceptance criteria. AC4/AC5 are regression guards against the wildcard-leakage exploits. AC6 guards the intentional bare-Write wildcard behavior. AC7/AC8 are e2e subprocess tests verifying the full hook pipeline produces correct sentinel JSON.

tasks_completed.description: Implement generated test stubs AC1-AC8 in tests/generated/20260524-125300-B/
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced TEST_INCOMPLETE pytest.fail() stubs with real implementations. Fixed HOOKS_DIR path from parent.parent.parent (resolves to tests/) to parent.parent.parent.parent (resolves to project root) so sys.path.insert correctly adds hooks/ to Python path.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex adversarial review found that generated stubs still hard-failed with TEST_INCOMPLETE. Implemented all 8 stubs so QA can run them directly.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — bugs introduced during original sentinel grant feature implementation
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Three interacting bugs: (1) _looks_regex() classifies dotted paths as regex, skipping sentinel write; (2) bare two-token Write+path form treats path as comment, producing wildcard sentinel; (3) writer emits args_contain but matcher keys on target, causing wildcard fallthrough. Together these allowed path-scoped /allow grants to silently become global Write grants.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Each fix targets the precise code path that introduced the escalation: force is_regex=False for --tool; detect Write+path bare form explicitly; emit correct field name in writer; use key-presence branching in matcher.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: 192 of 231 added lines are new test methods covering AC4-AC8 in the consolidation test file. The 4 code fix hunks in allowlist.py (31 lines changed) and userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (20 lines changed) sum to 51 total lines changed — within the minimum reasonable for 4 distinct fix locations across 2 files. Tests are required by the acceptance criteria and Codex adversarial review found the generated stubs still failing.

dev.qa_notes: Run: python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py tests/generated/20260524-125300-B/ -v. All 54 tests pass. Key regression guards: AC7 (e2e --tool dotted filename produces target-scoped sentinel, not wildcard) and AC8 (e2e bare syntax produces target-scoped sentinel). AC5 guards against path-prefix substring matching exploit. AC6 guards intentional bare-Write wildcard behavior is preserved.

blast_radius_declarations.reason: behavioral_test_only:true — posttool-allowlist-consume.py not modified this cycle. Covered by AC3 which tests consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result directly.

self_verification.notes: All 54 tests pass: python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py tests/generated/20260524-125300-B/ -v (54 passed, 0.69s). Verified AC7 and AC8 subprocess tests invoke the actual hook binary and confirm sentinel JSON contains op==Write and target==/tmp/file.json for both --tool flag and bare syntax. Python syntax verified implicitly via pytest import/collection.

recommendations: Add a conftest.py to tests/generated/ that sets HOOKS_DIR via project-relative discovery to avoid future path-depth bugs when new test directories are nested
Consider a lint rule that rejects _looks_regex() calls when the caller is handling --tool flag input (always explicit literal)
Add bash quoting linter for python3 -c "..." blocks to catch double-quote-in-comment bugs at authoring time

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex ran pytest and found 46 passed / 8 failed — the generated stubs in tests/generated/20260524-125300-B/ still had TEST_INCOMPLETE hard-fails. All four code fixes (is_regex override, bare Write+path detection, target vs args_contain writer, key-presence matcher) were verified correct. PROPOSED_FIX: implement the generated stubs. Also flagged that the bash double-quote comment fix was the critical subtlety explaining why AC7/AC8 previously failed silently.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Implemented all 8 generated test stubs (AC1-AC8) in tests/generated/20260524-125300-B/. Fixed HOOKS_DIR path depth from parent*3 to parent*4 in all stubs. All 54 tests now pass.
